patches.find: Biblical exegesis; a copper dome would be radar-detectable.
patches.replace: Biblical exegesis; a copper dome would block all radio astronomy and contradict its own Schumann resonance prediction.
patches.rationale: Finding text says 'radar-detectable' but evidence now uses radio astronomy impossibility and Schumann cross-reference. Finding should match the evidence.

patches.find: Deriving a mathematical relationship from assumed geometry and then testing it against measured data amounts to testing the model's internal consistency, not testing the model against nature.
patches.replace:
Deriving a mathematical relationship from assumed geometry and then testing it against measured data amounts to testing the model's internal consistency, not testing the model against nature.

Moreover, the eclipse predictions (E001–E009) that would test the FSF formula against independent INTERMAGNET data are all still pending — the August 12, 2026 eclipse has not yet occurred. Claiming a formula as a 'confirmed WIN' before the observations that would test it is circular: the formula is confirmed because it was derived, not because it was tested.
patches.rationale: WIN-044 claims FSF as confirmed WIN but the observational test (Aug 2026 eclipse) hasn't happened yet. This is the strongest available argument.

patches.find: Internal model derivation, not independent prediction; tests self-consistency, not truth
patches.replace: Internal model derivation claimed as confirmed WIN before observational test (eclipse August 2026); tests self-consistency, not truth
patches.rationale: Strengthens finding to capture both problems: internal derivation AND premature confirmation claim.

patches.find: z ~ 0 to z ~ 20 with thousands of supernovae, galaxies, and GRBs
patches.replace: z = 0.001 to z = 2.26 with 1,701 supernovae (Pantheon+), extended to z ~ 8 with gamma-ray bursts
patches.rationale: z ~ 20 is factually wrong. Pantheon+ covers z = 0.001 to 2.26. Even GRBs only reach z ~ 8-9. No Hubble diagram calibrator exists at z ~ 20.

patches.find: The dome model has no mechanism for generating redshift (no photon-aether interaction theory, no propagation equations) and no explanation for the observed isotropy of Hubble Law across all directions.
patches.replace: The dome model has no mechanism for generating redshift (no photon-aether interaction theory, no propagation equations) and no explanation for the observed isotropy of Hubble Law across all directions. Furthermore, the dome's firmament extends to ~9,086 km, yet galaxies are observed at millions of light-years. The dome has no ontology for extragalactic objects — without one, claims about their redshift behavior are physically meaningless.
patches.rationale: Adds fundamental structural objection: dome geometry cannot contain galaxies, so redshift claims about them are vacuous.
